description:
## Fix Applied

### Changes Made

1. **Added client tracking to EventBroadcaster** (src/websocket/event_broadcaster.cr):
   - Added @@client_history array to track client count over time
   - Added log_client_stats method that logs every heartbeat (30s)
   - Added client_count and client_history_summary methods
   - Logs WARN at >50 clients, ERROR at >100 clients

2. **Fixed WebSocket client lifecycle** (src/quickheadlines.cr):
   - Added EventBroadcaster.add_client(ws) on connection
   - Added EventBroadcaster.remove_client(ws) on close
   - Clients were not being removed from @@clients array

### Testing

Deploy the new binary and monitor logs:
```bash
# Watch for client count warnings
tail -f /var/log/quickheadlines.log | grep -i "client count\|EventBroadcaster"

# Check RSS growth over time
ps -o pid,rss,vsz -p $(pgrep quickheadlines)
```

### If Leak is EventBroadcaster
- Client count will grow unbounded (100+ after a few hours)
- Logs will show escalating warnings
- Fix: properly remove clients on disconnect (done above)

### If Leak Continues After Fix
- Suspect other sources: StateStore snapshots, actor mailboxes, fibers
- Add memory profiling instrumentation
- Consider restart-based leak detection
title: Memory growth - 9GB VIRT after 1.5h runtime
